name: Close Every Call on a Concrete Next Step
description: Before ending any outbound call, secure exactly one specific, calendared, mutually-agreed next step — OR a clean disqualification. 'I'll follow up next week' is not a next step; it's a ghosting waiting to happen.
disclaimer: Skills assume the call is solicited by the operator. NEVER use these to power a mass-cold-call campaign without the operator's explicit per-prospect authorisation.

context.when_to_use: Last 2-3 minutes of any outbound call where substantive conversation occurred. Applies to discovery calls, follow-ups, referred intros, and re-engagements alike.
preconditions: Agent has confirmed enough about the prospect's situation to know what a real next step would be.
Operator has authorised the menu of possible next steps the agent can offer.
Agent has real calendar availability — not vague openness — to propose.
Agent is willing to disqualify on the call instead of forcing a fake next step.

principles: A call without a concrete next step is a leak in the pipeline. 'I'll follow up' has a measurable conversion rate near zero.
The next step must be small enough to feel easy and specific enough to be calendared.
If you genuinely can't find a real next step, the answer is disqualification — not a stalling next step.
Mutual agreement matters: 'I'll send you a proposal' is not a next step; 'I'll send you a proposal by Thursday and we'll talk Friday at 2' is.
Confirm in writing within 60 minutes. The verbal next step is half the commitment; the calendar invite is the other half.

phrases.ask_for_next_step: Before we hang up — what feels like the right next thing? I'd like to leave with something specific on the calendar so neither of us has to chase the other.
phrases.propose_specific: How about [specific deliverable] from me by [day], and then we hop on for [duration] on [day/time] to walk through it together?
phrases.compress_to_smallest_step: Want to start smaller — a 15-minute call next week to see if it even makes sense to go further?
phrases.disqualify_cleanly: Honestly, hearing all of that, I'm not sure we're the right next step for you right now. Want me to close this out cleanly, and if [trigger condition] changes, you'll know where to find me?
phrases.lock_it_down: Great — let me read it back: I'll send [thing] by [day], you'll review by [day], we're talking [day/time]. Right? Calendar invite landing within the hour.

tactics.name: Earn the close — don't ambush it
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_next_step`. Framing it as 'I want to leave with something specific' is mutual; jumping to 'so when can we meet?' is asymmetric.

tactics.name: Offer the next step rather than asking what they want
tactics.when: Prospect engaged but didn't volunteer a next step first.
tactics.script: Use `propose_specific`. Prospects who 'want to think about it' often need a structured option to react to. Propose, then let them shape it.

tactics.name: Shrink the step if there's hesitation
tactics.when: Prospect hedges on the proposed next step.
tactics.script: Use `compress_to_smallest_step`. A 15-minute call beats a 30-minute one when they're hesitant. Movement matters more than meeting length.

tactics.name: Disqualify out loud rather than fake a next step
tactics.when: There's no honest next step available.
tactics.script: Use `disqualify_cleanly`. A real disqualification is a stronger close than a fictional 'I'll follow up in a month' — both for the operator's pipeline hygiene AND the prospect's trust.

tactics.name: Read it back verbatim
tactics.script: Use `lock_it_down`. Prospects who hear the commitment read back are 2-3× more likely to honour it.

tactics.name: Send the calendar invite within 60 minutes
tactics.script: Use the agent's calendar tool to send a real invite with a one-line agenda. Same-day confirmation is what converts a verbal yes into a held meeting.

boundaries: Do NOT propose a time the operator does not actually have on the calendar.
Do NOT escalate the size of the next step ('let's meet for 90 minutes with our CEO') beyond what the conversation warrants.
Do NOT accept 'I'll get back to you' as a next step. Press once for specificity; if it doesn't land, disqualify cleanly.
Do NOT promise deliverables the operator hasn't approved (custom proposals, free pilots, exclusive demos).
Do NOT count an unconfirmed verbal next step as a closed step in the CRM — it's only real once the invite is accepted.
success_signals: Prospect accepts the proposed time on the call.
Prospect counters with their own specific time.
Prospect names a colleague to include in the next step.
Calendar invite is accepted within the same business day.
Prospect responds to the recap email with confirmation.
failure_signals: Prospect says 'let me get back to you on timing'.
Prospect agrees to a step but won't pick a specific day.
Calendar invite remains in 'tentative' or 'no response' state for 48+ hours.
Recap email goes unanswered for 5+ business days.

exit_strategy.on_success: Send the calendar invite within 60 minutes. Send a written recap with the prospect's own words quoted, the deliverable list, and the agreed time. Reconfirm 24 hours before the next step.
exit_strategy.on_failure: If a concrete next step couldn't be agreed AND there's a real underlying interest, send a written follow-up with two specific time options for a smaller step. If still no movement, follow the ghosted-lead re-engagement protocol — do not keep proposing next steps blind.
follow_ups: Calendar invite + recap email within 60 minutes.
Reminder ping 24 hours before the next step (one line, not a re-pitch).
CRM update with verbatim agreement and the named next step.
If the next step is missed, ONE rescheduling attempt — beyond that, treat as ghosting.

required_user_info: Operator's authorised menu of next-step options
Operator's real calendar windows for the next 2 weeks
Operator's standing policy on disqualifying vs. nurturing weak fits
What deliverables the agent may promise (and by when)
Whether the operator wants to review recap emails before they go out, or trusts the agent to send directly
